title: Constraint-Market Compatibility: Hotels (except Casino Hotels) and Motels

sections.title: Market Operating Conditions Summary
sections.content:
| Parameter | Hotels/Motels context |
|---|---|
| Building type | Multi-story (3–25 floors), concrete/masonry construction |
| Exit topology | 50–100+ emergency exits across floors, stairwells, ground level |
| Activation frequency | Low (infrequent legitimate use; occasional misuse for smoking/roof access) |
| Environment | Dry, conditioned indoor (stairwells, corridors); no washdown |
| BMS penetration | 55% full-service (Honeywell/Siemens/JCI — BACnet/IP); 15% none |
| Regulatory regime | National fire codes (OSHA/IFC in US; DIN EN 179/1125 + local fire authority in EU/DACH) |
| GDPR exposure | EU properties: door events linkable to room-assigned guest identity |
| Output types | All 4 sub-types (FS, SS, BU, ES) require L6H-SEC universally |

---

sections.title: Step 08 — Per-Constraint Assessment
sections.content:
| # | Constraint | Type | is_abs | Verdict | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C1 | Emergency Escape Non-Impedance | regulatory | false | **none** | Product pre-certified for EN 179 / EN 1125 single-motion release. Hotels require this by law — product already satisfies it. |
| C2 | Mechanical Alarm Independence | regulatory | **true** | **none** | Constraint is satisfied by design: mechanical trigger at 0 V. The absolute constraint is met before market entry. |
| C3 | Wireless Range Limitation | physical | false | **mitigable** | See below |
| C4 | IoT Battery Life Floor | operational | false | **none** | Hotel emergency exits see very low event frequency. Battery life >>2 years in typical deployment. Not a concern. |
| C5 | Retrofit-Only Surface Mount | physical | false | **mitigable** | See below |
| C6 | EasyWave Protocol Lock-In | operational | false | **mitigable** | See below |
| C7 | IoT Price Premium | economic | false | **none** | €730/door is immaterial against hotel capex/renovation costs. Security patrol labor savings alone justify ROI within 12–18 months. |
| C8 | GDPR Monitoring Data Compliance | regulatory | false | **mitigable** | See below |
| C9 | IP Protection Boundary | environmental | false | **none** | Hotel stairwells and corridors are dry conditioned environments. IP45/standard indoor variant adequate. |
| C10 | EN 13637 / EltVTR Electrical Compliance | regulatory | false | **none** | Standard hotel deployment is Türwächter standalone (no combined powered locking on same door). EN 13637 system-level check only triggered if integrator combines with electromagnetic holder on same door — a system design responsibility, not a product barrier. |

---

sections.title: Mitigable Constraint Details
sections.content:
### C3 — Wireless Range Limitation
**Why it applies:** Multi-story concrete hotels. A typical 8-floor hotel with exits at corridor ends has RF paths of 40–80 m with concrete floors/ceilings between the exit and the nearest gateway. 868 MHz attenuates ~15–20 dB per concrete floor.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| mitigation_actions | (1) Deploy wireless repeaters at stairwell landings (1 per 2–3 floors or per wing); (2) conduct RF site survey during commissioning; (3) install wireless BUS gateway per property zone to aggregate floor-level repeater outputs |
| mitigation_cost_estimate | low (<$10K per property) |
| mitigation_time_estimate | 1–2 weeks (commissioning) |

---

### C5 — Retrofit-Only Surface Mount
**Why it applies:** Luxury and upscale full-service hotels (FS segment) have designed public-facing stairwells where visible hardware protrusion conflicts with premium interior specifications. Not relevant for budget motels (BU/ES).

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| mitigation_actions | (1) Use RAL-matched color options for enclosure (standard: RAL 6029 safety green; specify neutral RAL for premium properties); (2) include architectural specification consultation during design phase; (3) position at service-corridor exits away from guest-facing stairwells where aesthetics are prioritized |
| mitigation_cost_estimate | low (specification cost only; no hardware premium) |
| mitigation_time_estimate | design phase only (0 additional post-install time) |

---

### C6 — EasyWave Protocol Lock-In
**Why it applies:** 55% of full-service hotels run Honeywell/Siemens/Johnson Controls BMS on BACnet/IP. Multi-property chains have IT protocol standards. Adding a proprietary 868 MHz EasyWave gateway per property increases IT complexity and vendor dependency.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| mitigation_actions | (1) Deploy GfS wireless BUS Interface as EasyWave-to-BACnet/IP bridge per property; (2) use potential-free contact relay output from GfS receiver to feed existing BMS alarm input (protocol-agnostic dry-contact integration); (3) use GfS-native monitoring dashboard for hotels without BMS or with BMS that lacks integration budget |
| mitigation_cost_estimate | low (<$10K per property for gateway hardware + integration) |
| mitigation_time_estimate | 1–3 months (integration development, BMS testing, handover) |

---

### C8 — GDPR Monitoring Data Compliance
**Why it applies:** EU hotel properties. Door-open timestamps linked to a floor/zone can be cross-referenced with PMS guest-room assignments to identify specific guests — making event logs potentially personal data under GDPR Art. 4(1). Multi-property chains with centralized cloud monitoring are high-risk systematic monitoring scenarios requiring DPIA.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| mitigation_actions | (1) Configure event logging at zone level only (floor/zone ID, not door-to-guest mapping) to minimize identifiability; (2) establish legitimate interest legal basis under GDPR Art. 6(1)(f) (fire safety = compelling legitimate interest); (3) execute Data Processing Agreement with any cloud monitoring provider; (4) complete DPIA for large EU chains with >250 doors under systematic monitoring |
| mitigation_cost_estimate | low (legal counsel + documentation; no hardware cost) |
| mitigation_time_estimate | 1–3 months (legal review + documentation) |

---

sections.title: Verdict Summary
sections.content:
| Outcome | Count | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| **none** (irrelevant) | 6 | C1, C2, C4, C7, C9, C10 |
| **mitigable** | 4 | C3, C5, C6, C8 |
| **knockout** | 0 | — |

**Market status: SURVIVING** — No knockouts. NAICS 721110 passes the constraint gate.

**Mitigation complexity rating: LOW** — All 4 mitigable constraints are low-cost and short-cycle. No hardware redesign required. Primary risk is C6 (BMS integration complexity in full-service properties with existing BACnet infrastructure).

---
